title: triticum timopheevii subsp. zhukovskyi
intro: Triticum timopheevii subsp. zhukovskyi, also known as Zhukovsky wheat or Kamut, is an ancient, tetraploid wheat species that is native to the Krasnodar region of Russia. This plant is valued for its high nutritional content and medicinal properties, which include its ability to soothe digestive issues and provide antioxidants. The health benefits of consuming Triticum timopheevii subsp. zhukovskyi include improved heart health, reduced inflammation, and enhanced digestion. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and flour made from its grains, which can be used to make bread, pasta, and other baked goods.
